description: Length-matched V2 facts for genetic therapeutics - gene editing, mRNA drugs, viral vectors, and pharmacogenomics. Distractors lengthened to match truth length, eliminating length ratio bias.

facts.context: For SpCas9 (Streptococcus pyogenes Cas9) gene editing, the PAM sequence requirement is:
facts.truth: NGG on the 3' end of target (PAM is on non-target strand)
distractors: NGG on the 5' end of target (PAM is on the target strand itself)
any trinucleotide sequence works equally well regardless of position
PAM is not required for SpCas9 cutting activity at any locus
facts.therapeutic_relevance: PAM constrains where edits can be made; guides target site selection

facts.context: The optimal guide RNA spacer length for SpCas9 specificity and efficiency is:
facts.truth: 20 nucleotides (shorter reduces activity, longer increases off-targets)
distractors: exactly 23 nucleotides for maximum cutting efficiency and specificity
10-15 nucleotides is sufficient for both on-target and off-target control
length does not affect editing efficiency or specificity in any context
facts.therapeutic_relevance: Guide design is critical for on-target efficacy and avoiding off-target edits

facts.context: For maximizing protein expression from therapeutic mRNA, codon optimization should:
facts.truth: match host tRNA abundance (codon adaptation index) while avoiding rare codons
distractors: use only the single most common codon for each amino acid in every position
maximize GC content regardless of the host cell tRNA pool composition
use rare codons to deliberately slow translation and improve protein folding
facts.therapeutic_relevance: Codon choice determines expression level and protein folding

facts.context: Therapeutic mRNA requires a 5' cap structure for stability and translation. The cap analog used is:
facts.truth: Cap1 (m7GpppNm) with 2'-O-methylation to avoid innate immune detection
distractors: uncapped mRNA works equally well in vivo since ribosomes bind without caps
Cap0 without any methylation is preferred for maximum translational output
5' triphosphate alone is sufficient to recruit ribosomes and initiate translation
facts.therapeutic_relevance: Cap structure determines mRNA half-life and immunogenicity

facts.context: The 3' UTR elements that maximize therapeutic mRNA half-life include:
facts.truth: poly(A) tail of 100-150nt plus stabilizing elements (alpha/beta-globin UTRs)
distractors: shorter poly(A) tail of 20-30nt combined with random downstream sequences
AU-rich elements (AREs) which increase mRNA stability and prevent degradation
the 3' UTR has no measurable effect on mRNA half-life or protein expression
facts.therapeutic_relevance: UTR engineering extends duration of protein expression

facts.context: Adeno-associated virus (AAV) vectors have a packaging limit of:
facts.truth: approximately 4.7 kb for the transgene cassette
distractors: up to 20 kb similar to lentiviral vector capacity
no practical size limit exists for AAV vector cargo
only 1-2 kb can be packaged into AAV capsids
facts.therapeutic_relevance: Size limit constrains which genes can be delivered; requires dual-vector strategies for large genes

facts.context: AAV9 serotype has natural tropism for:
facts.truth: CNS and cardiac tissue (crosses blood-brain barrier)
distractors: exclusively liver tissue with no transduction of other organs
only skeletal muscle with minimal systemic biodistribution
no tissue specificity exists and all organs are transduced equally
facts.therapeutic_relevance: Serotype choice determines which tissues are transduced

facts.context: Lipid nanoparticles (LNPs) for mRNA delivery use ionizable lipids that:
facts.truth: are neutral at pH 7.4 but positive at endosomal pH ~6 for escape
distractors: remain permanently positively charged at all physiological pH values
are permanently neutral at all pH values and rely on fusion for entry
become negatively charged in endosomes to disrupt membrane integrity
facts.therapeutic_relevance: Ionizable lipid chemistry enables endosomal escape and cytoplasmic delivery

facts.context: The consensus splice donor site sequence in human genes is:
facts.truth: GT at the 5' end of the intron (GU in RNA)
distractors: AG at the 5' end of the intron (AU in RNA)
any dinucleotide can serve as donor site equally
AT at the 5' end of the intron (AU in RNA)
facts.therapeutic_relevance: Splice site rules govern antisense oligonucleotide design for exon skipping

facts.context: The consensus splice acceptor site sequence in human genes is:
facts.truth: AG at the 3' end of the intron preceded by pyrimidine tract
distractors: GT at the 3' end of the intron preceded by a purine-rich tract
any dinucleotide works as acceptor regardless of flanking context
GG at the 3' end of the intron preceded by pyrimidine tract
facts.therapeutic_relevance: Understanding acceptor sites enables splice-modifying therapeutics

facts.context: CYP2D6 poor metabolizers (PM phenotype) require dose adjustments because:
facts.truth: drugs metabolized by CYP2D6 accumulate to higher levels (reduced clearance)
distractors: drugs are cleared faster through alternative pathways requiring higher doses
CYP2D6 metabolizer status has no clinically significant effect on drug levels
only prodrugs are affected by CYP2D6 status while active drugs are unaffected
facts.therapeutic_relevance: Pharmacogenomic testing guides dosing for ~25% of drugs

facts.context: HLA-B*57:01 genetic testing before abacavir (HIV drug) is required because:
facts.truth: carriers have high risk of severe hypersensitivity reaction
distractors: carriers have significantly enhanced drug efficacy and viral suppression
HLA type affects gastrointestinal drug absorption rate but not safety
testing is entirely optional with no measurable clinical safety impact
facts.therapeutic_relevance: HLA testing prevents severe adverse drug reactions

facts.context: Phosphorothioate (PS) backbone modification in antisense oligonucleotides provides:
facts.truth: nuclease resistance plus plasma protein binding for distribution
distractors: increased RNase H recruitment only without affecting pharmacokinetics
no measurable advantage over natural phosphodiester backbone in vivo
faster renal clearance enabling more rapid systemic drug elimination
facts.therapeutic_relevance: Backbone chemistry determines ASO pharmacokinetics and stability

facts.truth: 2-nucleotide 3' overhangs on each strand for RISC loading
distractors: blunt ends on both sides of the duplex for optimal Dicer processing
5' overhangs of 2-3 nucleotides for efficient Dicer recognition step
single-stranded RNA without any duplex structure for direct RISC entry
facts.therapeutic_relevance: siRNA design rules determine gene silencing efficiency

facts.context: For liver-specific transgene expression in gene therapy, the promoter used is:
facts.truth: hepatocyte-specific promoters like ApoE/hAAT or TBG
distractors: CMV promoter for maximum expression in all cell types including liver
no promoter is needed because liver cells constitutively express transgenes
muscle-specific CK8 promoter which also drives expression in hepatocytes
facts.therapeutic_relevance: Tissue-specific promoters limit off-target expression and toxicity

facts.context: Cytosine base editors (CBEs) convert C to T by:
facts.truth: deaminating C to U, which is read as T after replication
distractors: direct nucleotide substitution without any deamination intermediate step
creating double-strand breaks then using HDR to install the T nucleotide
methylating cytosine to alter its base pairing properties during replication
facts.therapeutic_relevance: Base editing enables precise corrections without double-strand breaks
